titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles Benson to Thomas Kirkpatrick, 22 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles Molyneux Benson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Charles Molyneux Benson (1877-1919) to Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick (1869-1954) regarding proposed changes to the Constitution of the Conjoint Board. While the Royal College of Surgeons of Ireland (RSCI) with all of the amendments advocated by the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) they agree these to move ahead with the agreed changes and review any problems that arise over time.
From 1910 until 1954 Thomas Kirkpatrick served as the registrar for the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland. He also served as the general secretary of the Royal Academy of Medicine in Ireland. He took a particular interest in what were then termed venereal diseases; to encourage his patients (many of whom were prostitutes) to attend, he held a clinic for women at a discreet early morning hour to facilitate anonymity.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Surgeons, Stephen's Green, Dublin, Ireland
Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland. Dublin Jan. 22nd 1916 To T.P.C. Kirkpatrick Esq FRCP
Registrar
Royal College of Physicians Dear Sir, I am directed by the President, Vice President &
Council to thank the Royal College of Physicians for the
sympathetic consideration which they have given to proposals
which, while they materially affect the interests of both
Colleges, also benefit the College School. While the President, Vice President & Council accept the
Amendments proposed by the College of Physicians to the
"Report of the Report Committee to Consider the Constitution
of the Conjoint Examining Boards", they desire to point
out that the omission of the financial clause makes
temporary rearrangements of the Examining Board difficult,
and places an annual charge on the College of Surgeons,
which, although not large, is still inequitable; and that
the Amendment dealing with the Examiners in Medicine
makes it necessary to declare the Chair of Medicine in
the College School vacant next May. This course is very distasteful to the Council, in spite
of the knowledge that the claims of the present Professor
to re-election will be fully considered by your College.
But the necessity of their Professor of Medicine being an
Examiner in that important subject must override
all other considerations. The President, Vice President & Council recognise, however,
that the Amendments criticised by them deal only with
matters secondary to the main object of the report: and
they are satisfied to wait until the scheme, as amended,
has been in force long enough to develop its weak points,
before submitting to your College proposals for its
improvement I am, Sir,
Yours faithfully C. M. Benson. F.R.C.S.I
Secretary to Council.
